evals.name: biomedical-eeg-adhd
evals.prompt: 我剛完成 screening，請幫我匯出參考文獻。Session 資料在 research-screen-workspace/iteration-2/biomedical-eeg-adhd/with_skill/outputs/ 裡面，step3_shortlist.json 和 step3_screening_results.md 都在那裡。請產生 APA 7 reference list、citation key 對照表、還有 BibTeX 檔案。輸出請放在指定的 outputs 資料夾。
evals.expected_output: Three files: step4_references_apa.md (APA 7 formatted, sorted alphabetically), step4_citation_keys.md (key → citation → title → year table), step4_references.bib (valid BibTeX entries). ~36 papers, biomedical domain with EEG/neurofeedback terminology.
assertions: All three output files exist: step4_references_apa.md, step4_citation_keys.md, step4_references.bib
The number of references in step4_references_apa.md matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of entries in step4_references.bib matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of rows in the citation key table matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
APA references are sorted as a single flat alphabetical list (not grouped by tier)
APA references use sentence case for titles (not Title Case)
APA references use author initials format (e.g., 'Author, A. B.'), not full given names
Every multi-author APA entry has & before the last author name
DOIs in APA references use https://doi.org/ prefix format
BibTeX uses @inproceedings for conference papers and @article for journal papers (not all @article or all @misc)
Citation keys use PascalCase AuthorYear convention (e.g., Zhang2024, XiaZhang2024, BouzeniaEtAl2025) — not lowercase
No citation key has a lone disambiguation suffix (e.g., no Zhang2024a without a Zhang2024b)
Every citation key in citation_keys.md appears exactly once in the .bib file
step4_references_apa.md contains bilingual headers (English + 繁體中文)
step4_references_apa.md includes YAML frontmatter with session_id, topic, date, step

evals.name: cs-llm-program-repair
evals.prompt: Screening is done for my LLM program repair literature review. Export the references now — the shortlist is at research-screen-workspace/iteration-2/cs-llm-program-repair/with_skill/outputs/step3_shortlist.json. I need APA references, citation keys, and a .bib file for LaTeX. Save outputs to the specified outputs folder.
evals.expected_output: Three files with ~40 CS papers. Conference papers (ICSE, ISSTA, FSE) should use @inproceedings in BibTeX. arXiv preprints should use @misc. Citation keys should handle multiple Zhang2024-style collisions with a/b/c disambiguation.
assertions: All three output files exist: step4_references_apa.md, step4_citation_keys.md, step4_references.bib
The number of references in step4_references_apa.md matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of entries in step4_references.bib matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of rows in the citation key table matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
APA references are sorted as a single flat alphabetical list (not grouped by tier)
APA references use sentence case for titles (not Title Case)
APA references use author initials format (e.g., 'Author, A. B.'), not full given names
Every multi-author APA entry has & before the last author name
DOIs in APA references use https://doi.org/ prefix format
BibTeX uses @inproceedings for conference papers and @article for journal papers (not all @article or all @misc)
Citation keys use PascalCase AuthorYear convention (e.g., Zhang2024, XiaZhang2024, BouzeniaEtAl2025) — not lowercase
Duplicate author-year combinations are disambiguated with a/b/c suffixes (e.g., Zhang2024a, Zhang2024b)
No citation key has a lone disambiguation suffix (e.g., no Zhang2024a without a Zhang2024b)
Every citation key in citation_keys.md appears exactly once in the .bib file
BibTeX entries protect proper nouns and acronyms with braces (e.g., {LLM}, {ChatGPT}, {Defects4J})
step4_references_apa.md contains bilingual headers (English + 繁體中文)

evals.name: cross-disciplinary-bci-depression
evals.prompt: 已經篩選完 BCI 憂鬱症的論文了，下一步請幫我 export references。資料在 research-screen-workspace/iteration-2/cross-disciplinary-bci-depression/with_skill/outputs/。需要 APA 格式的參考文獻清單、citation key 表、跟 BibTeX 檔。輸出到指定 outputs 資料夾。
evals.expected_output: Three files with ~29 papers spanning neuroscience, psychiatry, and engineering. Should handle mixed venue types (journals + conferences + preprints). Cross-disciplinary author names from multiple naming conventions.
assertions: All three output files exist: step4_references_apa.md, step4_citation_keys.md, step4_references.bib
The number of references in step4_references_apa.md matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of entries in step4_references.bib matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
The number of rows in the citation key table matches the number of papers in step3_shortlist.json
APA references are sorted as a single flat alphabetical list (not grouped by tier)
APA references use sentence case for titles (not Title Case)
APA references use author initials format (e.g., 'Author, A. B.'), not full given names
Every multi-author APA entry has & before the last author name
DOIs in APA references use https://doi.org/ prefix format
BibTeX uses correct entry types: @inproceedings for conferences, @article for journals, @misc for preprints
Citation keys use PascalCase AuthorYear convention (e.g., Zhang2024, XiaZhang2024, BouzeniaEtAl2025) — not lowercase
No citation key has a lone disambiguation suffix (e.g., no Zhang2024a without a Zhang2024b)
Every citation key in citation_keys.md appears exactly once in the .bib file
step4_references_apa.md contains bilingual headers (English + 繁體中文)
step4_citation_keys.md includes a Tier column showing the paper's screening tier
